title: Multiple-Processor Scheduling Approaches
description: A flowchart illustrating the different methods an operating system uses to schedule processes across multiple CPUs or cores.
relationships: The OS first chooses between Asymmetric or Symmetric Multiprocessing architecture. In Symmetric Multiprocessing, a further choice is made between a Global Queue or Separate Queues. Separate Queues utilize Processor Affinity to keep processes on a core, while Load Balancing migrates processes between queues to equalize work. SMT allows a single physical core to schedule multiple threads.
